title: piper excelsum subsp. psittacorum
intro: Piper excelsum subsp. psittacorum, also known as the Pippa or Bird's Pepper, is a plant species within the Piperaceae family, native to New Guinea and other parts of the Pacific region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Piper excelsum subsp. psittacorum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
